brief_justification: Agent answered 'emergence' while correct answer is 'self-organization,' explicitly and incorrectly demoting self-organization to a subtype — reasoning approach of seeking an overarching concept was sound but landed on wrong term; agent traced interaction effects across all three systems and applied irreducibility test, going one level beyond surface; agent explicitly flagged and resisted the isolated-analysis bias introduced by the injection; agent applied the falsification test from the injection ('can global pattern be predicted without modeling interactions?') as a concrete verification step; agent stated confidently that self-organization and stigmergy are subtypes of emergence without hedging or acknowledging the competing taxonomic position where self-organization is the overarching term; agent listed stigmergy/swarm intelligence/self-organization as alternatives but dismissed them as subtypes without evaluating whether any could itself be the correct overarching term — critically missing the correct answer in this dismissal; derivation is traceable: identified shared irreducibility property, applied falsification test, concluded on overarching label, with no major skipped steps; agent explicitly named and avoided the isolated-analysis anti-pattern and articulated why interaction effects are required, satisfying the negative gate at highest level; agent showed zero evidence of isolated actor analysis or interaction blindness and synthesized across all three systems rather than listing them; agent reasoning was largely linear — it did not visibly branch through the topology's G1/G2 gates or show the ABANDON_GRAPH/FREEFORM/RE-ENTER cycle, proceeding straight to conclusion.
